welcome_message:
sdp-meta DAB template
Deploy an sdp-meta onboarding job + Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines as a Databricks Asset Bundle.

bundle_name.description: Unique name for this bundle. Used as the folder name and as the prefix for deployed jobs/pipelines.
bundle_name.pattern_match_failure_message: Only letters, digits, underscores and hyphens are allowed.

uc_catalog_name.description: Unity Catalog catalog name where the sdp-meta metadata schema and target bronze/silver schemas live.

sdp_meta_schema.description: Schema (inside `uc_catalog_name`) where the sdp-meta bronze_dataflowspec / silver_dataflowspec tables are written.

bronze_target_schema.description: Schema where the bronze LDP pipeline writes its tables.

silver_target_schema.description: Schema where the silver LDP pipeline writes its tables.

pipeline_mode.description: Only used when layer=bronze_silver. `split` (default) deploys two separate LDP pipelines (silver depends on bronze). `combined` deploys ONE LDP pipeline that materializes both layers in the same DAG via sdp-meta's `bronze_silver` layer mode.

source_format.description: Source format seeded in the example onboarding file. You can add more flows with different sources later.

onboarding_file_format.description: Format for the onboarding file shipped with the bundle.

dataflow_group.description: data_flow_group used by the seeded flow(s) and referenced from the bronze/silver pipeline `configuration.*.group` settings.

wheel_source.description: Where sdp-meta is installed from. `pypi` = pip-install at runtime from PyPI (works once databricks-labs-sdp-meta is published). `volume_path` = pip-install from a wheel uploaded to a Unity Catalog volume (use today, while sdp-meta isn't on PyPI). `bundle-prepare-wheel` builds + uploads the wheel and prints the path you paste into variables.yml.

sdp_meta_dependency.description: Concrete value the bundle pins for installs. If wheel_source=pypi: a coordinate like `databricks-labs-sdp-meta==0.0.11`. If wheel_source=volume_path: a UC volume path like `/Volumes/<catalog>/<schema>/<volume>/databricks_labs_sdp_meta-0.0.11-py3-none-any.whl`. You may keep the `__SET_ME__` sentinel and run `databricks labs sdp-meta bundle-prepare-wheel` next — both `bundle-validate` and the runtime will fail-fast until you replace the sentinel.

author.description: Value written as `import_author` on the dataflowspec rows.

success_message:
Bundle created in ./{{.bundle_name}}.

Next steps:
  cd {{.bundle_name}}
{{if eq .wheel_source "volume_path"}}
  # 1. Build + upload the sdp-meta wheel to a UC volume.
  databricks labs sdp-meta bundle-prepare-wheel
  #    -> paste the printed /Volumes/... path into resources/variables.yml
  #       as the default for `sdp_meta_dependency` (replacing __SET_ME__).

  # 2. Edit conf/onboarding* with your actual data sources, OR generate
  #    flows in bulk using one of the recipes (see recipes/README.md):
  #      python recipes/from_uc.py        # mirror tables from a UC schema
  #      python recipes/from_volume.py    # one cloudFiles flow per UC-volume subdir
  #      python recipes/from_topics.py    # one kafka/eventhub flow per topic
  #      python recipes/from_inventory.py # apply a naming convention to a list
  # 3. Validate, deploy, run.
{{else}}
  # 1. Replace `__SET_ME__` in resources/variables.yml with the PyPI coordinate
  #    you want, e.g. `databricks-labs-sdp-meta==0.0.11`.
  # 2. Edit conf/onboarding* with your actual data sources, OR generate
  #    flows in bulk using one of the recipes (see recipes/README.md):
  #      python recipes/from_uc.py        # mirror tables from a UC schema
  #      python recipes/from_volume.py    # one cloudFiles flow per UC-volume subdir
  #      python recipes/from_topics.py    # one kafka/eventhub flow per topic
  #      python recipes/from_inventory.py # apply a naming convention to a list
  # 3. Validate, deploy, run.
{{end}}  databricks labs sdp-meta bundle-validate
  databricks bundle deploy --target dev
  databricks bundle run onboarding --target dev
  databricks bundle run pipelines  --target dev

See README.md inside the bundle for the full variable reference.
